message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  In this preliminary report, PCR positivity data in the second wave of the COVID pandemic (September-January 2020) are shown to obey a scaling law given by:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn1">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="21267073v1_ueqn1.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                  where % P
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  and Σ
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  are the y- and x-intercepts of a plot of positivity, %P, against the number of tests, Σ. The law holds across international, regional and local boundaries, as demonstrated for Great Britain, Austria, Germany and Sweden, the nine English regions, London - Yorkshire & Humber, and various Local Health Authorities in England. One possible explanation for scaling might be Dorfman pooling.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The scaling law can be used to remove a systematic or false positive (FP) component from the daily number of positive tests, or cases, to yield the real number of cases. The results correlate strongly with the ZOE survey for London (R
                  <jats:sup>2</jats:sup>
                  =0.787) and Excess Deaths for England (R
                  <jats:sup>2</jats:sup>
                  =0.833). The cumulative total of FPs can be estimated as 1.4M by the beginning of 2021, in line with other estimates.
                </jats:p>
title: A Scaling Law for PCR Positivity in the COVID Second Wave

reference.unstructured: A Ohnishi et al, medRxiv preprint https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.06.18.20135210; see also references 1-20 therein.
M Yeadon , C Craig et al., ‘PCR Testing’, Briefing for MPs, 28.11.2020.
CDC 2019-Novel Coronavirus (2019-nCoV) Real-Time RT-PCR Diagnostic Panel: Instructions for Use, Catalog # 2019-nCoVEUA-01, 21.07.2021.
COVID-19 FACT SHEET FOR HEALTHCARE PROVIDERS, Roche Molecular Systems, Inc., 15.10.2020.
NHS Standard operating procedure: Pooling of asymptomatic SARS COV-2 COVID-19 samples for (PCR/or other) testing, Publications approval reference: 001559, 25.09.2020.
J Verwilt et al, medRxiv preprint, https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.07.17.20152702.
N Shental et al., Sci. Adv. 2020; 6: eabc5961, 11.09,2020.
See for instance D Spiegelhalter in BBC Radio 4, ‘More or less’, 27.09.2020.

R Davies , Final Year Dissertation,Sheffield Hallam University, May 2021; https://app.powerbi.com/viewr=eyJrIjoiYmUwNmFhMjYtNGZhYS00NDk2LWFlMTAtOTg0OGNhNmFiNGM0IiwidCI6ImVlNGUxNDk5LTRhMzUtNGIyZS1hZDQ3LTVmM2NmOWRlODY2NiIsImMiOjh9.
